([Portuguese](/wiki/Portuguese_language "Portuguese language"): *Catedral Metropolitana de São Sebastião*), better known as the *Metropolitan Cathedral of Rio de Janeiro* (*Catedral Metropolitana do Rio de Janeiro*) or as the **Cathedral of St. Sebastian of Rio de Janeiro** (*Catedral de São Sebastião do Rio de Janeiro*), is a [cathedral](/wiki/Cathedral "Cathedral") of the [Catholic Church](/wiki/Catholic_Church "Catholic Church") and the [seat](/wiki/Episcopal_see "Episcopal see") of the [Archdiocese of Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Archdiocese_of_Rio_de_Janeiro "Archdiocese of Rio de Janeiro"). The cathedral is home to the [cathedra](/wiki/Cathedra "Cathedra") of the [Archbishops](/wiki/Archbishop "Archbishop") of the city of [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro"), [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil"). The church is dedicated to [Saint Sebastian](/wiki/Saint_Sebastian "Saint Sebastian"), the [patron saint](/wiki/Patron_saint "Patron saint") of Rio de Janeiro.

The **Metropolitan Cathedral of Saint Sebastian** ([Portuguese](/wiki/Portuguese_language "Portuguese language"): *Catedral Metropolitana de São Sebastião*), better known as the *Metropolitan Cathedral of Rio de Janeiro* (*Catedral Metropolitana do Rio de Janeiro*) or as the **Cathedral of St. Sebastian of Rio de Janeiro** (*Catedral de São Sebastião do Rio de Janeiro*), is a [cathedral](/wiki/Cathedral "Cathedral") of the [Catholic Church](/wiki/Catholic_Church "Catholic Church") and the [seat](/wiki/Episcopal_see "Episcopal see") of the [Archdiocese of Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Archdiocese_of_Rio_de_Janeiro "Archdiocese of Rio de Janeiro"). The cathedral is home to the [cathedra](/wiki/Cathedra "Cathedra") of the [Archbishops](/wiki/Archbishop "Archbishop") of the city of [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro"), [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil"). The church is dedicated to [Saint Sebastian](/wiki/Saint_Sebastian "Saint Sebastian"), the [patron saint](/wiki/Patron_saint "Patron saint") of Rio de Janeiro.

## Architecture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rio_de_Janeiro_Cathedral&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Architecture")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/ac/Rio_de_janeiro_cathedral_sao_sebastiao_2010.JPG/250px-Rio_de_janeiro_cathedral_sao_sebastiao_2010.JPG)](/wiki/File:Rio_de_janeiro_cathedral_sao_sebastiao_2010.JPG)

Inside the Cathedral

The cathedral was designed by Edgar de Oliveira da Fonseca in a modern style based on [Mayan architectural style](/wiki/Maya_architecture "Maya architecture") of [pyramids](/wiki/Mesoamerican_pyramids "Mesoamerican pyramids").[[1]](#cite_note-1) The current cathedral was built between 1964 and 1979 and replaced as seat of the Archdiocese a series of churches that had served as cathedrals since 1676, the most recent and notable of those being the [Church of Our Lady of Mount Carmel of the Ancient See](/wiki/Old_Cathedral_of_Rio_de_Janeiro "Old Cathedral of Rio de Janeiro"), now known as the *Old Cathedral*, built in the 18th century, and that had been declared Rio's cathedral in the early 19th century.

The *New Cathedral*, as it is sometimes called, is located in the center of the city. Conical in form and with a 96 metres (315 ft) internal diameter — 106 metres of external diameter — and an overall height of 75 metres (246 ft). Inside, the area measures 8,000 square meters and sufficient 5,000 seats (it has a standing-room capacity of 20,000 people).

The cathedral's four [rectilinear](/wiki/Rectilinear_polygon "Rectilinear polygon") [stained glass](/wiki/Stained_glass "Stained glass") windows soar 64 metres (210 ft) from floor to ceiling.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4f/Interior_Panorama_Rio_de_Janeiro_Cathedral_Sao_Sebastiao.JPG/960px-Interior_Panorama_Rio_de_Janeiro_Cathedral_Sao_Sebastiao.JPG)](/wiki/File:Interior_Panorama_Rio_de_Janeiro_Cathedral_Sao_Sebastiao.JPG)

Interior panorama of the Cathedral São Sebastião
